2605.19064.title: Toward an AI-Powered Computational Testbed for Workforce Policy
2605.19064.paper_summary: Workforce transformations are difficult to forecast and costly to mismanage. In particular, the integration of artificial intelligence into knowledge work currently affects a substantial share of the global workforce, yet this transition proceeds without tools to forecast how individual employees will respond psychologically and behaviorally. We combine recent advances in LLM-powered generative agents with foundational management science and organizational behavior research to propose dynamic employee agents. Among consenting populations, these agents can be seeded with HR records, validated psychometric measures, and digital activity data to simulate employees' cognitive, emotional, and behavioral trajectories across successive workdays during planned organizational changes. In this article, we detail the computational architecture required to construct this simulation platform and define the privacy, accuracy, and representativeness safeguards necessary for responsible deployment. We argue that establishing this prospective forecasting infrastructure is a critical technical requirement for managing the current global workforce realignment around AI.
2605.19064.paper_summary_zh: 勞動力轉型難以預測，且錯誤管理的成本高昂。<lb/>特別是，人工智慧在知識工作中的整合目前影響著全球勞動力的相當一部分，但這一過渡卻在缺乏預測個別員工心理和行為反應的工具的情況下進行。<lb/>我們結合了最近在大型語言模型驅動的生成代理方面的進展與基礎管理科學和組織行為研究，提出了動態員工代理。<lb/>在同意的群體中，這些代理可以利用人力資源記錄、經過驗證的心理測量指標和數位活動數據來模擬員工在計劃中的組織變革期間，隨著工作日的推移，認知、情感和行為的軌跡。<lb/>在本文中，我們詳細說明了構建這一模擬平台所需的計算架構，並定義了負責任部署所需的隱私、準確性和代表性保障措施。<lb/>我們主張，建立這一前瞻性預測基礎設施是管理當前全球勞動力因人工智慧而重新調整的關鍵技術要求。
2605.19064.author: Sumer S. Vaid et.al.
2605.19064.authors: Sumer S. Vaid, Ashley V. Whillans
